deep_dive.title: Differentially Private Submodular Maximization with a Knapsack Constraint
deep_dive.abstract: Submodular maximization subject to a knapsack constraint (SMK) is a fundamental problem in discrete optimization, with wide-ranging applications in machine learning and related fields. As these applications increasingly involve sensitive individual data, there is a growing need for high-utility algorithms that provide formal privacy guarantees. In this work, we study the SMK problem under differential privacy, considering both monotone and non-monotone objective functions. For monotone objectives, we propose a differentially private algorithm that achieves the optimal $(1-1/e)$-approximation ratio while significantly improving both additive error and query complexity over prior work. We also present a more efficient algorithm for the same setting, achieving a $1/2$-approximation. For non-monotone objectives, we introduce, to our knowledge, the first differentially private algorithm with provable guarantees, achieving a $1/4$-approximation in expectation and an additive error comparable to the best known for monotone objective functions.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Differential privacy for submodular maximization optimization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向子模最大化优化的差分隐私算法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向子模最大化优化的差分隐私算法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向子模最大化优化的差分隐私算法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes DP algorithms for monotone and non-monotone submodular maximization with knapsack constraint, achieving optimal approximation with improved error.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对带背包约束的子模最大化问题，本文提出了满足差分隐私的算法。对于单调目标，算法达到最优的(1-1/e)近似比，同时显著降低了加性误差和查询复杂度；对于非单调目标，也给出了高效的私有算法。相关工作填补了差分隐私在离散优化领域的重要空白。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对带背包约束的子模最大化问题，本文提出了满足差分隐私的算法。对于单调目标，算法达到最优的(1-1/e)近似比，同时显著降低了加性误差和查询复杂度；对于非单调目标，也给出了高效的私有算法。相关工作填补了差分隐私在离散优化领域的重要空白。

deep_dive.title: Multi-tier Differential Private Query Release
deep_dive.abstract: Answering statistical queries over sensitive data under differential privacy (DP) is a common task in many settings, including databases, mobile computing, and data markets. In these scenarios, multiple analysts may issue the same query, while receiving answers generated under different privacy budgets due to differences in trust levels or willingness to pay. Existing approaches for such multi-tier DP queries either incur excessive cumulative privacy loss or suffer from suboptimal utility. In this paper, we propose a framework for multi-tier DP query release that simultaneously bound the cumulative privacy loss by the maximum privacy budget among all queries and achieve optimal utility comparable to that of single-tier mechanisms. Our framework applies to different classes of DP mechanisms. For noise-adding mechanisms (e.g., count queries with the two-sided Geometric mechanism in the curator model), we develop a general solution based on the characteristic functions of noise distributions. For other mechanisms (e.g., count queries under the local DP model with the Subset mechanism), we design mechanism-specific primitives for budget transformation and introduce a template-based strategy that attains optimal utility across different privacy regimes. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: differential privacy for multi-tier query release
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 差分隐私多层级查询发布
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 差分隐私多层级查询发布
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 差分隐私多层级查询发布
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a framework for multi-tier differential private query release that bounds cumulative privacy loss by the maximum budget and achieves optimal utility comparable to single-tier mechanisms, applicable to various query classes.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对多层级差分隐私查询中隐私损失累积过大或效用不足的问题，提出一个统一框架，同时限制隐私损失不超过最大隐私预算并达到与单层机制相当的最优效用。该框架适用于不同查询类别，为多信任级别下的安全数据查询提供了高效解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对多层级差分隐私查询中隐私损失累积过大或效用不足的问题，提出一个统一框架，同时限制隐私损失不超过最大隐私预算并达到与单层机制相当的最优效用。该框架适用于不同查询类别，为多信任级别下的安全数据查询提供了高效解决方案。

deep_dive.title: Conflict-Aware Federated Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts
deep_dive.abstract: The continuous scaling of large language models (LLMs) incurs prohibitive computational costs, making Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) a scalable alternative for efficient fine-tuning via sparse activation. While federated learning (FL) emerges as the paradigm for privacy-preserving collaborative optimization, integrating MoE into FL under data heterogeneity may trigger conflicting expert optimizations. Client-specific data distributions force same-indexed experts to optimize under inconsistent or even conflicting feature-label correlations. This mismatch induces destructive interference during aggregation, thus destabilizing the optimization trajectory and degrading model performance. To address this issue, we propose FC-MoE, a federated conflict-aware framework for MoE fine-tuning. It employs an importance aware weighting scheme to prioritize reliable local updates and utilizes gradient consensus projection to suppress conflicting updates, ensuring a stable global optimization path. Moreover, a local knowledge retention mechanism further preserves specialized client expertise by re-anchoring domain-specific residuals. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FC-MoE accelerates convergence and enhances both global and local model performance in non-IID federated environments.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Federated fine-tuning with MoE addressing expert conflicts for privacy-preserving distributed ML
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过MoE解决专家冲突的联邦微调，实现隐私保护的分布式机器学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过MoE解决专家冲突的联邦微调，实现隐私保护的分布式机器学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过MoE解决专家冲突的联邦微调，实现隐私保护的分布式机器学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes FC-MoE for federated fine-tuning of LLMs with mixture-of-experts, addressing client-specific expert conflicts via conflict-aware aggregation to stabilize training and improve performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 在联邦学习环境下，混合专家（MoE）模型因客户端数据异质性导致相同索引专家产生冲突优化，破坏全局模型性能。本文提出FC-MoE，一种冲突感知的联邦微调方法，通过识别聚合过程中的专家冲突并施加调整，抑制破坏性干扰。实验表明，FC-MoE在多个异构数据场景下显著提升了模型性能，为隐私保护的分布式机器学习提供了一种鲁棒且高效的微调方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 在联邦学习环境下，混合专家（MoE）模型因客户端数据异质性导致相同索引专家产生冲突优化，破坏全局模型性能。本文提出FC-MoE，一种冲突感知的联邦微调方法，通过识别聚合过程中的专家冲突并施加调整，抑制破坏性干扰。实验表明，FC-MoE在多个异构数据场景下显著提升了模型性能，为隐私保护的分布式机器学习提供了一种鲁棒且高效的微调方案。

deep_dive.title: Differential Privacy of Gaussian Process Posterior Sampling
deep_dive.abstract: We study the privacy of releasing posterior sample paths from a Gaussian process (GP) when the entire training set including covariates and responses is private. Unlike standard differential-privacy (DP) mechanisms that add external noise, posterior sampling is random by construction. We show that this intrinsic randomness yields DP guarantees by deriving explicit Rényi-DP bounds for GP posterior sample-path release. The bounds separate posterior-mean leakage from data-dependent posterior-covariance leakage showing that meaningful privacy depends sharply on effective ridge regularisation. We apply membership-inference attacks to show that empirical leakage follows the predicted dependence on regularisation, posterior variance and the number of released posterior sample-paths. Utility experiments on downstream posterior-sampling tasks identify noisy-observation regimes where privacy-compatible regularisation preserves useful decisions with modest utility loss. When stronger privacy is needed, the intrinsic guarantee can be sharpened by adding calibrated GP noise, providing an explicit additional privacy knob.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: differential privacy of Gaussian process posterior sampling
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 高斯过程后验采样的差分隐私分析
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 高斯过程后验采样的差分隐私分析
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 高斯过程后验采样的差分隐私分析
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Derives explicit Rényi-DP bounds for Gaussian process posterior sample-path release, showing that intrinsic randomness provides privacy guarantees depending on ridge regularization, validated by membership inference attacks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究高斯过程后验采样的内在随机性能否自然提供差分隐私保证。通过推导后验样本释放的显式Rényi-DP边界，分离了均值泄漏与协方差泄漏，发现隐私水平强烈依赖于有效岭正则化。成员推断攻击实验证实了理论预测，表明在适当正则化下后验采样本身即可满足差分隐私，无需添加外部噪声。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文研究高斯过程后验采样的内在随机性能否自然提供差分隐私保证。通过推导后验样本释放的显式Rényi-DP边界，分离了均值泄漏与协方差泄漏，发现隐私水平强烈依赖于有效岭正则化。成员推断攻击实验证实了理论预测，表明在适当正则化下后验采样本身即可满足差分隐私，无需添加外部噪声。

deep_dive.title: Federated Bilevel Performative Prediction
deep_dive.abstract: Federated bilevel optimization is widely used for nested learning problems across distributed clients, such as federated hyperparameter tuning and meta-learning under privacy and communication constraints. Most existing formulations assume fixed client data distributions, which can be violated by performativity, where deployed decisions reshape client behavior and data collection, inducing client-specific, decision-dependent distribution shift. We study federated bilevel performative prediction, where both upper-level (UL) and lower-level (LL) objectives are evaluated under client-dependent, decision-dependent distributions. We formalize the federated bilevel performatively stable (FBPS) point under a decoupled-risk perspective and provide sufficient conditions for its existence and uniqueness. We then develop two federated methods to compute the FBPS solution: FBi-RRM, which converges linearly under a contraction condition, and FBi-SGD, a communication-efficient stochastic method based on federated hypergradient estimation with convergence guarantees under diminishing step sizes when sensitivities are sufficiently small. Experiments on strategic regression and meta strategic classification validate the predicted stability thresholds and demonstrate improved meta-generalization over non-performative baselines, and CNN-based classification further demonstrates the practical effectiveness of the proposed methods in nonconvex neural network settings.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: federated bilevel optimization with privacy and communication constraints
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 隐私和通信约束下的联邦双层优化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 隐私和通信约束下的联邦双层优化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 隐私和通信约束下的联邦双层优化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Studies federated bilevel performative prediction where client distributions shift based on deployed decisions, formalizing the federated bilevel performatively stable point and providing existence conditions under decoupled risk.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究联邦双层优化中决策影响客户数据分布的绩效性问题，现有工作假设固定分布不成立。作者定义了联邦双层绩效稳定点（FBPS），在解耦风险视角下给出其存在性的充分条件。理论结果揭示了客户特异性分布偏移下依然存在稳定解，为隐私和通信约束下的联邦学习提供了更现实的优化框架。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文研究联邦双层优化中决策影响客户数据分布的绩效性问题，现有工作假设固定分布不成立。作者定义了联邦双层绩效稳定点（FBPS），在解耦风险视角下给出其存在性的充分条件。理论结果揭示了客户特异性分布偏移下依然存在稳定解，为隐私和通信约束下的联邦学习提供了更现实的优化框架。

deep_dive.title: A-COMPASS: Formal Foundations for Anonymity Analysis in Microdata
deep_dive.abstract: In the information age, one of the leading problems is how to ensure individual's privacy. Depending on the context in which privacy is considered, various data privacy models have emerged. However, the domain of formal verification of these models is still not sufficiently explored even when it comes to the most basic models. An attempt to verify privacy requirements is the Compliance Assertion Language (COMPASS). In COMPASS, one can specify an anonymity condition that a table needs to satisfy, and an action that will modify the table if the condition is not satisfied. It is designed to operate on preprocessed tables in a form one record - one group of people. In this paper, we modify the COMPASS language in order to operate on microdata tables in their usual form of one record - one person. The modified language is called A-COMPASS. Along with checking of previously applied anonymity conditions, A-COMPASS enables the execution of anonymization actions as a new feature. We further provide the syntax and the semantics for the A-COMPASS language. We also prove the most important properties of the introduced semantics like determinism and compositionality. Finally, we provide a mechanism to verify anonymity properties, such as k-anonymity and l-diversity.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Formal framework for anonymity analysis in microdata
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 微数据匿名性分析的形式化框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 微数据匿名性分析的形式化框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 微数据匿名性分析的形式化框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Extends the COMPASS language to operate on microdata tables, providing formal verification for anonymization conditions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对微数据表的匿名性验证问题，本文改进了COMPASS语言，使其能够直接操作原始记录级别的微数据表，并支持指定匿名条件和修正动作。该工作为微数据隐私保护提供了形式化分析基础，增强了隐私模型的可验证性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对微数据表的匿名性验证问题，本文改进了COMPASS语言，使其能够直接操作原始记录级别的微数据表，并支持指定匿名条件和修正动作。该工作为微数据隐私保护提供了形式化分析基础，增强了隐私模型的可验证性。

deep_dive.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
deep_dive.abstract: Differential privacy (DP) ensures rigorous individual-level privacy guarantees against even the most knowledgeable attackers, but its worst-case nature can impose a costly privacy-accuracy tradeoff. We introduce privacy via predictability, a fine-grained framework that explicitly incorporates the attacker's core knowledge, a compromised portion of the dataset generated by a stochastic process, and a specified family of queries. Predictability measures privacy leakage as the incremental gain in an attacker's ability to predict sensitive information about unknown individuals after observing the algorithm's output, beyond what can already be inferred from the compromised data. We show that predictability and DP are generally incomparable: each can be small while the other is large. However, in the worst-case regime where all but one individual is compromised, and all binary queries are considered sensitive, predictability implies mutual-information DP. More generally, predictability provides a finer-grained privacy metric tailored to specific sensitive information and specific attacker models. We introduce a general framework, using the generalized method of moments (GMM), to analyze asymptotic predictability when the compromised data is generated by a stationary, ergodic, mixing process. Using this analysis, we derive a predictability-calibrated output perturbation scheme for ERM. Our approach is complementary to DP and can be used alongside DP to provide fine-grained privacy control.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Novel fine-grained privacy measure called predictability
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出名为可预测性的细粒度隐私度量
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出名为可预测性的细粒度隐私度量
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出名为可预测性的细粒度隐私度量
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces predictability, a privacy metric that incorporates attacker's prior knowledge and measures incremental prediction gain, shown to be incomparable with differential privacy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对差分隐私的严格最坏情况假设导致隐私-效用权衡过重的问题，本文提出基于可预测性的细粒度隐私框架。该框架明确考虑攻击者先验知识，衡量算法输出对敏感信息预测能力的增量提升。实验表明可预测性与差分隐私不可比较，且能实现更好的隐私-效用平衡。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对差分隐私的严格最坏情况假设导致隐私-效用权衡过重的问题，本文提出基于可预测性的细粒度隐私框架。该框架明确考虑攻击者先验知识，衡量算法输出对敏感信息预测能力的增量提升。实验表明可预测性与差分隐私不可比较，且能实现更好的隐私-效用平衡。

quick_skim.title: Achieving Precise Text-To-Cypher Via Grounded Knowledge Graph Data Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Property Graphs are rapidly being adopted as database frameworks for representing heterogeneous data sources. To enable precise access to the information contained in them we need conversational interfaces based on Text-To-Cypher (Text2Cypher) parsers. This paper presents an automatic synthetic data generation method that can be leveraged to fine-tune small LLMs for this task. We conduct experiments on all the major Text-To-Cypher benchmarks, demonstrating that with our synthetic data generation approach we can significantly increase the performance of small LLMs, allowing them to compete with much larger proprietary models. This means that in settings in which models must be locally deployed we can ensure data-sovereignty without sacrificing accuracy and without costly annotation campaigns.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Text-to-Cypher via grounded KG data generation for fine-tuning small LLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过基于知识图谱的数据生成微调小型大语言模型实现文本到Cypher
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过基于知识图谱的数据生成微调小型大语言模型实现文本到Cypher
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过基于知识图谱的数据生成微调小型大语言模型实现文本到Cypher
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents an automatic synthetic data generation method for fine-tuning small LLMs to perform Text-to-Cypher, enabling competition with large proprietary models while preserving data sovereignty.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对属性图数据库的Text-to-Cypher任务，本文提出了一种基于知识图谱的自动合成数据生成方法，用于微调小型大语言模型。在多个主流基准上，该方法使小型模型性能显著提升，达到与大型专有模型相当的水平。这保证了在本地部署场景下的数据主权，无需牺牲查询准确性，为数据库自然语言接口提供了一种高效、低成本的实现方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对属性图数据库的Text-to-Cypher任务，本文提出了一种基于知识图谱的自动合成数据生成方法，用于微调小型大语言模型。在多个主流基准上，该方法使小型模型性能显著提升，达到与大型专有模型相当的水平。这保证了在本地部署场景下的数据主权，无需牺牲查询准确性，为数据库自然语言接口提供了一种高效、低成本的实现方案。

quick_skim.title: CONCORD: Asynchronous Sparse Aggregation for Device-Cloud RAG under Document Isolation
quick_skim.abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has emerged as a pivotal technique for improving language models by incorporating external knowledge at inference time. As device-cloud collaborative inference makes it feasible to deploy small language models on edge devices, a new setting arises in which private documents remain on the device and public knowledge resides in the cloud. Privacy and policy constraints often forbid raw document exchange, creating a document-isolated dual-end RAG setting. However, existing methods rely on frequent remote synchronization and dense evidence transfer, limiting throughput under realistic latency and bandwidth conditions. To address this issue, we propose CONCORD, an asynchronous sparse aggregation framework for dual-end RAG under document isolation. CONCORD treats the cloud as an asynchronously arriving evidence source rather than a continuously synchronized co-generator. Specifically, we introduce waiting debt control to decide whether each decoding step should continue waiting for remote participation based on the observed return of waiting. We also design a certificate-guided minimal supplementation mechanism that requests only the remote evidence needed to determine the current greedy decision. Steps that consult the cloud preserve the same greedy token as dense dual-end aggregation, while the remaining steps commit locally without remote evidence. Experiments on Natural Questions and WikiText-2 show that CONCORD improves end-to-end throughput over baselines by $1.66\times$ and $2.15\times$, respectively, while reducing per-token communication by over two orders of magnitude and maintaining comparable answer quality and perplexity.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: document isolation and privacy constraints in device-cloud RAG
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 设备-云RAG中的文档隔离与隐私约束
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 设备-云RAG中的文档隔离与隐私约束
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 设备-云RAG中的文档隔离与隐私约束
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CONCORD proposes asynchronous sparse aggregation for device-cloud RAG under document isolation, preserving privacy and improving throughput.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对设备-云协同检索增强生成中的文档隔离与隐私约束问题，本文提出CONCORD异步稀疏聚合框架，通过异步通信和稀疏证据传输，在保护隐私的同时大幅提升吞吐量。实验表明该方法在延迟和带宽受限条件下显著优于现有同步密集传输方案，为隐私保护型分布式RAG系统提供了一种高效解决方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对设备-云协同检索增强生成中的文档隔离与隐私约束问题，本文提出CONCORD异步稀疏聚合框架，通过异步通信和稀疏证据传输，在保护隐私的同时大幅提升吞吐量。实验表明该方法在延迟和带宽受限条件下显著优于现有同步密集传输方案，为隐私保护型分布式RAG系统提供了一种高效解决方案。

quick_skim.title: Verifiable User Simulation for Search and Recommendation Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Large-language-model (LLM) based user simulation is increasingly adopted for evaluating search engines, recommender systems, and retrieval-augmented generation pipelines, yet most simulators remain opaque: it is difficult to determine why a simulated user made a particular choice or whether that choice is consistent with the intended user profile. Compounding this, recent research shows that LLMs can produce biased or discriminatory responses depending on user background characteristics such as language, education level, and cultural context, raising concerns about the equitable treatment of minority and disadvantaged groups. This half-day, in-person tutorial introduces a proposed design-and-audit framework that treats a user simulator as a verifiable engineering artefact composed of seven auditable components - structured Persona, task-aware Contract, matched human-vs-agent Execution, auditable Trace, persona-aligned Verification, structured Feedback, and a Refinement loop that updates personas and contracts. Through two hands-on mini-labs on recommendation-list evaluation and search-query formulation, participants will inspect simulator behaviour end-to-end, distinguish diagnostic discrepancy analysis from statistical validation, and apply checks for fidelity, credibility, and demographic bias. The tutorial targets information retrieval and recommender systems researchers and practitioners interested in user behaviour simulation and responsible AI.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Verifiable user simulation for search and recommendation systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于搜索和推荐系统的可验证用户模拟
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于搜索和推荐系统的可验证用户模拟
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于搜索和推荐系统的可验证用户模拟
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a design-and-audit framework for verifiable LLM-based user simulation, ensuring consistency with user profiles and addressing bias, for evaluating search and recommendation systems.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对基于大语言模型的用户模拟器缺乏可解释性和可能存在的偏见问题，本文提出了一个设计与审计框架。该框架将用户模拟器视为可验证引擎，能够确保模拟行为与目标用户画像一致，并检测与人口统计特征相关的歧视。教程中详细介绍了如何设计模拟器以及如何审计其公平性，为搜索和推荐系统的可信评估提供了方法论支持。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对基于大语言模型的用户模拟器缺乏可解释性和可能存在的偏见问题，本文提出了一个设计与审计框架。该框架将用户模拟器视为可验证引擎，能够确保模拟行为与目标用户画像一致，并检测与人口统计特征相关的歧视。教程中详细介绍了如何设计模拟器以及如何审计其公平性，为搜索和推荐系统的可信评估提供了方法论支持。

quick_skim.title: PreLort: Prefix-Nested LoRA for Federated Fine-Tuning under Rank Heterogeneity
quick_skim.abstract: Federated fine-tuning of large language models using parameter-efficient methods such as LoRA enables privacy-preserving adaptation of foundation models. Heterogeneous hardware resources introduce challenges, as clients with different adapter ranks cannot be directly aggregated. While existing methods enable aggregation under heterogeneous ranks, they fail to control how information is distributed across rank dimensions, leading to suboptimal use of shared low-rank representations. Instead, we propose PreLort: a nested low-rank formulation for federated LoRA that organizes adapter dimensions into a prefix hierarchy. Our approach ensures that lower-rank dimensions encode task-relevant information, while higher-rank dimensions capture additional capacity. Building on this, we introduce (i) a segment-wise aggregation rule that averages only over clients contributing to each rank segment, avoiding dilution from zero-padded lower-rank clients, and (ii) a prefix-nested training strategy that optimizes each adapter under multiple rank truncations, encouraging useful signal to concentrate in low-rank prefix dimensions. Together, these components encourage a consistent low-rank prefix capturing the most task-relevant information, while higher-rank dimensions learn additional capacity. This allows low-rank clients to benefit from richer information contributed by higher-rank clients, as prefix dimensions are consistently learned and aggregated. Experiments demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms prior heterogeneous federated LoRA methods in accuracy and ROUGE-L, while achieving lower or comparable perplexity across multiple base models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 隐私保护的联邦微调LoRA方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 隐私保护的联邦微调LoRA方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 隐私保护的联邦微调LoRA方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PreLort introduces a prefix-nested low-rank formulation for federated LoRA, enabling privacy-preserving aggregation under heterogeneous ranks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对联邦大模型微调中客户端秩异构导致聚合困难且隐私保护不足的问题，本文提出PreLort前缀嵌套低秩框架，通过将适配器维度组织成前缀层次，确保低秩维度编码任务相关信息，高秩维度捕获额外细节，实现异构秩下的高效隐私保护聚合。实验证明该方法在保持模型性能的同时显著减少通信开销。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对联邦大模型微调中客户端秩异构导致聚合困难且隐私保护不足的问题，本文提出PreLort前缀嵌套低秩框架，通过将适配器维度组织成前缀层次，确保低秩维度编码任务相关信息，高秩维度捕获额外细节，实现异构秩下的高效隐私保护聚合。实验证明该方法在保持模型性能的同时显著减少通信开销。

quick_skim.title: G-Lox: Group-Adaptive, Privacy-Preserving Bridge Distribution with Two-Party Computation
quick_skim.abstract: We present G-Lox (group-adaptive Lox), a bridge-distribution system that preserves Lox-style distributor blindness while enabling hidden, stateful group-level adaptation. G-Lox places adaptive assignment logic behind a two-server privacy wall, so no single server learns group identifiers or group-to-bridge assignments. Private state access and state-dependent updates use two-server DPF/FSS protocols and secure two-party computation, supporting blockage reporting, transport-aware reassignment, and privacy-preserving group splitting.   We evaluate G-Lox through system measurements and policy simulation. In our C++/EMP implementation over real TCP sockets, private state access has low client-visible overhead: across state sizes up to 2^16, communication remains in the low-KiB range per iteration. At M=1024, the client sends 1,968 bytes, receives 1,280 bytes, and completes an iteration in about 0.25 s. Simulations with group-specific blocking and Sybil enumeration show that G-Lox improves robustness over Lox- and rBridge-like baselines among systems that maintain broad issuance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: privacy-preserving two-party computation for distributed bridge distribution
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 隐私保护的两方计算用于分布式桥梁分发
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 隐私保护的两方计算用于分布式桥梁分发
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 隐私保护的两方计算用于分布式桥梁分发
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: G-Lox is a privacy-preserving bridge distribution system using two-server DPF/FSS and secure two-party computation to maintain distributor blindness while enabling stateful group adaptation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有桥梁分发系统难以同时保护隐私和支持自适应分配。G-Lox提出一种组自适应的隐私保护架构，将自适应逻辑置于两方计算后，利用DPF/FSS协议实现私有状态访问，支持阻塞报告和传输感知重分配。实验表明客户端开销低，为分布式隐私系统提供了新方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有桥梁分发系统难以同时保护隐私和支持自适应分配。G-Lox提出一种组自适应的隐私保护架构，将自适应逻辑置于两方计算后，利用DPF/FSS协议实现私有状态访问，支持阻塞报告和传输感知重分配。实验表明客户端开销低，为分布式隐私系统提供了新方案。

quick_skim.title: Policy-aware Vector Search: A Vision for Fine Grained Access Control in Vector Databases
quick_skim.abstract: Vector databases are increasingly used in security sensitive contexts with Retrieval Augmented Generation and organizational AI pipelines; however, their security capabilities remain limited. Specifically, Fine-grained Access Control (FGAC) which is required to ensure that data access adheres to user-specific policies is not fully supported in modern vector databases. Unlike relational databases, vector databases combine structured and unstructured attributes to provide semantic, approximate query results, which complicates FGAC implementation. This creates an inherent tension between enforcing FGAC policies correctly, achieving high ANN search recall and maintaining low query latency. In this paper, we present a vision for Policy-aware Vector Search by formalizing the FGAC policy model in vector databases as well as the enforcement problem. We compare various enforcement strategies, present preliminary findings, and identify key open challenges for future research in policy-aware vector search.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Policy-aware vector search for fine-grained access control in ML pipelines
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于机器学习管道细粒度访问控制的策略感知向量搜索
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于机器学习管道细粒度访问控制的策略感知向量搜索
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于机器学习管道细粒度访问控制的策略感知向量搜索
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a vision for policy-aware vector search integrating fine-grained access control with high recall and low latency in vector databases, essential for privacy in distributed ML.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 向量数据库在检索增强生成（RAG）等ML管道中广泛使用，但现有系统缺乏细粒度访问控制（FGAC），难以在保障准确性和低延迟的同时执行用户特定策略。本文提出了Policy-aware Vector Search的愿景，通过将访问控制策略嵌入向量搜索过程，在保持高召回率和低查询延迟的前提下实现FGAC。该工作为分布式机器学习中的隐私数据访问控制提供了新思路，是构建可信AI基础设施的关键一步。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 向量数据库在检索增强生成（RAG）等ML管道中广泛使用，但现有系统缺乏细粒度访问控制（FGAC），难以在保障准确性和低延迟的同时执行用户特定策略。本文提出了Policy-aware Vector Search的愿景，通过将访问控制策略嵌入向量搜索过程，在保持高召回率和低查询延迟的前提下实现FGAC。该工作为分布式机器学习中的隐私数据访问控制提供了新思路，是构建可信AI基础设施的关键一步。
